basic_info.name: Sumbawa Lakey Peak Cluster - Lakey Peak, Lakey Pipe, Periscopes, Nungas, Cobblestones (Chinese: 松巴哇島雷基峰浪點群 - 雷基峰/雷基管浪/潛望鏡/努嘉斯/鵝卵石; Indonesian: Lakey Peak / Pantai Lakey - Hu'u, Dompu)
basic_info.gps_location: Lakey Peak village: -8.8456, 118.2772; Lakey Peak break: -8.8463, 118.2768; Lakey Pipe: -8.8451, 118.2785; Periscopes: -8.8389, 118.2756; Nungas (Nunga Doro): -8.8528, 118.2647; Cobblestones: -8.8617, 118.2453 (approx.). Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/LakeyPeakHuuSumbawa
basic_info.distance_from_dps_airport_km: ~420-440 km straight-line east of DPS; total travel ~5-7 hrs via flight DPS-BMU (Sultan Muhammad Salahuddin Airport, Bima) 1 hr 10 min on Garuda/TransNusa/Lion Air, then 2-3 hr drive (~110 km) southwest from Bima to Lakey Peak via Dompu
basic_info.distance_from_canggu_uluwatu_min: Not applicable directly - requires inter-island transfer; from Canggu/Uluwatu motorbike to DPS airport 60-90 min then flight DPS-BMU 1 hr 10 min + ground transfer 2-3 hrs

surf_conditions.wave_type: Multi-spot cluster around Hu'u Bay: Lakey Peak (world-renowned A-frame reef peak with peeling left and shorter punchier right; one of the most photographed A-frames in surfing); Lakey Pipe (heavy left-hand reef tube - best stand-up barrels in Indonesia when Peak is too big); Periscopes (right-hand reef break at northern end of Lakey reef - punchy wall and barrel section over shallow reef/rock platform); Nungas / Nunga Doro (long left-hand reef break that links sections into 200m+ rides at 8 ft+); Cobblestones (horseshoe bay with right on north side and left on south side requiring high tide)
surf_conditions.skill_level: Mostly Intermediate to Advanced/Pro; Lakey Peak A-frame holds intermediates on small days but becomes advanced when 4 ft+; Lakey Pipe and Periscopes are advanced/pro only (shallow reef, hollow); Nungas advanced on size; Cobblestones offers slightly mellower right for intermediates on small swell
surf_conditions.wave_height_range: Lakey Peak: 2-10 ft (holds all sizes, best 3-8 ft); Lakey Pipe: 3-8 ft (mid-to-high tide barrels); Periscopes: 2-8 ft; Nungas: 4-10+ ft (becomes world-class at 8 ft+ when sections connect); Cobblestones right works small swell, left needs high tide and can get sketchy
surf_conditions.bottom_type: Sharp coral reef at all primary breaks; Periscopes has reef/rock platform; Cobblestones has the namesake cobblestone/rock bottom on its bay sides; reef becomes shallow and dangerous at low tide especially at Lakey Pipe
surf_conditions.best_season: Dry season May-October (peak June-September; August often classic); offshore SE/E trade winds dominate; Sumbawa is generally consistent year-round but wet season Nov-Apr brings onshore winds and rain; Lakey Peak itself is regarded as one of the most consistent waves in Indonesia
surf_conditions.best_tide: Lakey Peak: mid tide ideal (holds all sizes); low tide produces extremely hollow barrels (left side); Lakey Pipe: mid-to-high tide only (low tide too shallow); Periscopes: mid tide; Nungas: mid to low tide for hollow sections; Cobblestones left needs high tide; refer to a tide chart - timing is critical for spot rotation
surf_conditions.best_wind: Offshore SE to E trade winds prevailing in dry season; afternoon onshore SW common; the various spots have slightly different orientations allowing spot rotation through the day - Periscopes can hold a bit more wind, Cobblestones tucked in bay is wind-protected
surf_conditions.best_swell_direction: S to SSW groundswell from deep Indian Ocean lows; SW also works; Lakey Peak picks up almost any south-facing swell; long-period swells 12-18s produce the cleanest A-frames
surf_conditions.swell_consistency_rating: 5 (Lakey Peak is regarded as one of the most consistent waves in Indonesia - some sources note 'consistent waves throughout the year' though peak is May-September); the cluster as a whole means there is almost always something rideable
surf_conditions.wave_length_meters: Lakey Peak: 80-150m on left, 30-60m on right (it's an A-frame peak, not a long point wave); Lakey Pipe: 50-100m intense barrel; Periscopes: 60-120m right; Nungas: 50-200m+ at 8 ft when sections link; Cobblestones: 60-150m depending on side
surf_conditions.wave_speed_rating: Lakey Peak: medium to fast (punchy A-frame); Lakey Pipe: very fast hollow tube; Periscopes: fast with punchy wall; Nungas: medium-fast with hot-dog walls and barrel sections; Cobblestones: medium
surf_conditions.takeoff_zone_difficulty: Lakey Peak: moderate to high (steep A-frame peak; on small days more approachable); Lakey Pipe: very high (ledging hollow take-off over shallow reef - reputation for hard-core reef cuts); Periscopes: high (punchy take-off over reef/rock platform); Nungas: high on size with shifting peaks; Cobblestones left slab section sketchy at end
surf_conditions.secondary_peaks: The whole area IS a multi-peak cluster - within 20 min radius of Lakey Peak Haven there are 9 named waves including the 5 above plus Salomons (or 'Sailoma'), Lakey Beach Break, and others; the cluster's strength is rotating between breaks based on swell/tide/wind
surf_conditions.peak_crowd_level: 4 at Lakey Peak in peak season (often crowded - total visiting surfers can hit 150-200 in early morning high tides, can be aggro for best conditions; 30+ years of surf history means 2nd-generation locals); other spots in cluster (Nungas, Periscopes, Cobblestones) typically less crowded - boat hops escape crowds

practical_info.access: Fly DPS to Bima (BMU) Sultan Muhammad Salahuddin Airport on Garuda/TransNusa/Lion Air (~1 hr 10 min flight); then 2-3 hr drive (~110 km) southwest from Bima via Dompu to Hu'u/Lakey Peak village; surf camps arrange pickup (IDR 500,000-600,000 per car/USD 32-40). Lakey Peak break is walk-in from the beach. Other breaks: Lakey Pipe walk along reef at low tide; Periscopes northern end of reef walk or short boat; Nungas/Cobblestones require boat or short drive plus paddle.
practical_info.paddle_out_method: Lakey Peak: walk in from beach across sand/reef shelf then paddle out to the A-frame peak; Lakey Pipe: walk along reef at low tide then paddle out (booties essential); Periscopes: walk to northern end of reef, paddle out over reef platform; Nungas: short boat or moto + paddle; Cobblestones: boat across or drive to horseshoe bay then paddle
practical_info.recommended_session_time: Dawn patrol (5:30-7:30 AM) for glassy offshore conditions and best wind window; second session after afternoon wind drops (4:00-6:00 PM); tide windows dictate which spot is rideable at any given hour - check tide chart daily
practical_info.boat_charter_required: Not strictly - Lakey Peak, Lakey Pipe, and Periscopes are walk-in from Lakey Beach. Boat charter required for Cobblestones and useful for Nungas; many surf camps offer boat day-trips between breaks (IDR 100,000-300,000 per person depending on operator)
practical_info.combo_trip_potential: Excellent - Sumbawa is a classic Bali extension trip; commonly combined with Bali (Bukit) + Lakey Peak 7-14 days; further chain to West Sumbawa (Scar Reef, Yo-Yos, Supersuck, Phantoms) via 6-8 hr overland drive west; boat charters from Bali Benoa to Sumbawa often include Lakey Peak as a stop; some operators do Lombok + Sumbawa overland via ferry
practical_info.alternative_spot_within_10min: Within the cluster itself - rotating between Lakey Peak, Lakey Pipe, Periscopes, Nungas, Cobblestones based on tide/wind/swell. The classic logic: if Peak is too crowded -> Cobblestones; if Nungas is mushy at high tide -> Periscopes

safety_culture.hazards: Sharp shallow coral reef (Lakey Pipe especially - hard-core reef cuts documented), strong currents on bigger days, crowded competitive lineups at peak, malaria-prone area (Sumbawa is one of Indonesia's higher-risk malaria regions - prophylaxis recommended), dengue, sunburn/dehydration in remote area with limited shade, occasional sea snakes, motorbike accidents on rough roads, slow medical evacuation
safety_culture.marine_hazards: Sea urchins on reef (booties essential at Lakey Pipe and Peak low tide), banded sea kraits possible (highly venomous but rarely aggressive), stonefish on reef (extremely venomous - immerse wound in hot water), reef cuts with very high tropical infection risk; sharks not a documented issue
safety_culture.lineup_etiquette: Respect 30+ years of local surf history - 2nd generation Sumbawan locals at Lakey Peak deserve priority on their wave; major etiquette rule at Lakey Peak A-frame: call your direction loudly at take-off (LEFT! or RIGHT!) since two surfers can drop on the same peak going opposite ways; do not snake; respect priority based on positioning; share waves; smile and chat onshore
safety_culture.localism_intensity: Medium - can be a bit aggro at Lakey Peak when conditions are firing and crowd is at 150-200; generally welcoming to respectful guests; Lakey Pipe and outside reefs see less crowd and lower localism intensity
safety_culture.respect_protocol: Be respectful to Sumbawan Muslim culture - dress modestly off-beach (cover shoulders and knees in villages and Dompu town), avoid public alcohol in conservative areas, observe Ramadan fasting hours discreetly during fasting month (warungs may close in daytime); tip surf-guide/boat captains; do not damage reef; engage with local community projects (Harapan Project healthcare in Hu'u)
safety_culture.resident_local_legends: Lakey Peak has produced and hosted generations of Sumbawan and Indonesian pros over its 30+ year history; numerous WSL pros and Indonesian legends have visited; specific names not documented in research but local surf-guide community at Lakey Peak Surf Guides and camps holds strong local knowledge [uncertain]
safety_culture.religious_sensitivity: Predominantly Sumbawan Muslim community (Sasak/Bimanese - mosques broadcast call to prayer five times daily); Hu'u village is conservative - dress modestly off-beach; Ramadan and Eid observance important - some warungs close during daytime fasting; respect local fishing community routines
safety_culture.medical_nearby: Lifeguard with first-aid equipment at Lakey Beach; small clinic in Rasabou ~20 min by moped (basic, more like a puskesmas); Dompu has a small hospital (RSUD Dompu) with some English-speaking doctors ~45-60 min drive away; Bima has a larger hospital ~2 hr drive; for serious cases a flight back to Bali (BIMC, Siloam Bali) is arranged; Harapan Project local healthcare organization operates in Hu'u
safety_culture.rescue_jet_ski_available: No - no permanent rescue jet ski; lifeguard with first-aid is available at Lakey Beach; some boat-charter operators have skis during big days but coverage is informal
safety_culture.emergency_contact: Indonesia general emergency: 112; nearest clinic Rasabou (basic puskesmas) ~20 min by moped; Dompu hospital RSUD Dompu ~45-60 min; serious cases evacuate to Bali via Bima flight (BMU-DPS); BIMC Bali +62 361 3000 911; satellite/cell signal limited - surf camps assist with arranging emergency transport

services.accommodation_options: Aman Gati Hotel Lakey (40 rooms, AC, pool, sea-view rooms and villas, in front of Lakey Peak - from ~USD 49/double, 7-night Bali-package from USD 470); Mona Lisa Bungalows (original 22-room budget seaside inn ~USD 15/night); The Peak Surf House (boutique surf camp); Lakey Peak Haven (mid-range surf camp near 5 world-class waves); Lakey Beach Inn; smaller homestays and losmen along Lakey Beach. Most camps offer surfboard storage, transfers from Bima, and meals.
services.webcam_url: Surfline Lakey Peak spot page: https://www.surfline.com/surf-report/lakey-peak/5842041f4e65fad6a7708d52 (forecast + spot guide); live cam coverage limited - some surf camps post daily social media reports [uncertain]
services.surf_photographer_available: Yes - long-running freelance and camp-based photographers cover Lakey Peak during peak season; Lakey Peak is one of the most photographed A-frames in surfing media; check social media or arrange via Aman Gati / The Peak / camp managers

pricing.avg_lesson_price: Not a primary lesson destination (mainly intermediate+); when offered, private surf guiding/coaching USD 30-80/day arranged informally through camps; Lakey Peak Surf Guides offers local guide services [uncertain on specific rates]
pricing.board_rental_daily: Limited rental at camps and a few local shops ~IDR 150,000-300,000/day for shortboards; most surfers bring own quiver (recommended 6'2-7'0 step-up for Lakey Pipe and bigger Peak days) [uncertain]

sustainability.water_quality_rating: Generally excellent - Hu'u Bay has no urban/industrial runoff and remains pristine; some seasonal sediment during wet-season storms; protected reef ecosystem with limited fishing pressure

events_community.annual_competition_window: Lakey Peak has hosted WSL QS and ASC (Asian Surfing Championships) events in past years; intermittent pro contest visits; not a regular CT stop; classic dawn-patrol communal surf-camp culture rather than formal contest season [uncertain on current schedule]
events_community.community_events: Dawn-patrol surf-camp dinners and Bintang nights; Sumbawan cultural events including local rodeo/buffalo racing in Dompu region; Ramadan/Eid observance; Harapan Project community healthcare events
